error_signature: You need to sign in or sign up before continuing.
title: Chatwoot API authentication failure due to Nginx configuration
symptom: API requests to Chatwoot return a '401 Unauthorized' status with a message indicating the need to sign in or sign up. This occurs even with valid credentials.
root_cause: Nginx's default configuration strips HTTP headers containing underscores, which are required by the Chatwoot API for authentication tokens. This results in the API not receiving the necessary authentication headers, leading to unauthorized access errors.
fix_steps: Access the CapRover Dashboard and navigate to the Chatwoot app.
Edit the Nginx configuration in the HTTP Settings tab.
Add the line 'underscores_in_headers on;' to the server block.
Save and update the configuration to apply changes.
Test the API connection again to ensure it works.
agent_instruction: Modify the Nginx configuration to allow headers with underscores and save the changes.
